executive_summary: A multi-agent system to transform the MCP knowledge server from a shallow index-reader into a rich, authoritative codebase intelligence layer. The core problem: agents fall back to Read/Grep/Glob because MCP tools return truncated summaries (e.g., 'data-loader module'), a 3800-byte response ceiling that loses context mid-symbol, no code snippets, substring-only search, stale tech-stack detection, and caller results without call-site context. Success is measured when agents stop issuing file reads for information the MCP already indexed.

observed_gaps.evidence: knowledge_context brief shows purpose strings like 'types module', 'data-loader module' — identical to the filename, providing zero value
observed_gaps.root_cause: LLM summarizer falls back to static heuristic (strip extension, append 'module') when summarization fails or is skipped
observed_gaps.agent_behavior: Agent ignores summary, issues Read tool call to see actual file content

observed_gaps.evidence: response-budget.ts MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES=3800; a single file with 10 symbols fills this, truncating remaining symbols and sections
observed_gaps.root_cause: Conservative budget set for latency reasons, but it destroys context completeness
observed_gaps.agent_behavior: Agent sees truncated symbol list, does follow-up Read to get the rest

observed_gaps.gap: No code snippets in any tool
observed_gaps.evidence: All tools return metadata only (name, signature, line number). No tool returns the actual function body or usage examples
observed_gaps.root_cause: Index stores AST-derived metadata, not source text slices
observed_gaps.agent_behavior: Agent must Read the file at the reported line to understand what the function actually does

observed_gaps.evidence: get_project_overview returns 'Languages: (unknown)' and 'Project Type: Unknown' for a TypeScript/Node.js project
observed_gaps.root_cause: tech-stack.ts heuristics don't detect tsconfig.json or .ts extensions correctly when package.json has no 'main' field
observed_gaps.agent_behavior: Agent cannot trust the overview, resorts to manual package.json inspection

observed_gaps.evidence: find_symbol.ts line 52: nameLower.includes(nameLower) — pure substring, case-insensitive only
observed_gaps.root_cause: No embedding index, no fuzzy matching, no alias or export-name resolution
observed_gaps.agent_behavior: Typos or partial names return no results; agent switches to Grep tool

observed_gaps.evidence: find_callers returns qualifiedName + file + line — no surrounding code, no argument values passed
observed_gaps.root_cause: Index stores qualified names only, not code slices at call sites
observed_gaps.agent_behavior: Agent reads caller file at reported line to understand how the symbol is invoked

observed_gaps.gap: No staleness awareness in tool responses
observed_gaps.evidence: health_check reports lastBuilt timestamp but individual tool responses carry no age metadata
observed_gaps.root_cause: Tools read index files synchronously without comparing mtime against source files
observed_gaps.agent_behavior: Agent cannot determine whether find_symbol result reflects latest code; may trust stale data or redundantly verify

observed_gaps.gap: Circular dependency detection but no resolution guidance
observed_gaps.evidence: knowledge brief notes '1 cycle(s)' but provides no details on which modules or how to reason about it
observed_gaps.root_cause: DependencyGraph stores cycles array but tools don't surface cycle membership in responses
observed_gaps.agent_behavior: Agent cannot understand module relationships without reading all import chains

observed_gaps.evidence: get_project_overview shows no npm packages, versions, or external API surface
observed_gaps.root_cause: Tech stack detection reads package.json name/main but not dependencies/devDependencies fields
observed_gaps.agent_behavior: Agent reads package.json directly to understand what libraries are available

observed_gaps.gap: find_symbol results do not include callers/callees inline
observed_gaps.evidence: Symbol result shows signature, returnType, jsdoc but not calls[] or calledBy[] from SymbolEntry
observed_gaps.root_cause: find_symbol handler omits calls and calledBy fields from output rendering
observed_gaps.agent_behavior: Agent calls find_callers as a mandatory second step after every find_symbol

observed_gaps.gap: get_module_context truncates file purposes to 60 chars
observed_gaps.root_cause: Aggressive truncation in combination with already-hollow summaries yields unusable descriptions
observed_gaps.agent_behavior: Agent calls get_implementation_context for every module file individually

observed_gaps.evidence: search_architecture is line-grep of a single architecture.md file; no vector-based search over all summaries
observed_gaps.root_cause: No embedding generation step in knowledge build pipeline
observed_gaps.agent_behavior: Agent falls back to Grep across source files for concept-level queries

current_state_analysis.trust_deficit_score: High — agents cannot rely on MCP tools for 8 of 12 common query patterns

trust_criteria.description: Observable, measurable signals that indicate agents prefer MCP tools over file reads

metrics.target: >=90% of agent turns that need codebase context use MCP tools as first call
metrics.measurement: Count MCP tool calls vs Read/Grep/Glob/Bash calls in agent logs per session

metrics.target: >=70% reduction in Read tool calls that immediately follow an MCP tool call on the same file
metrics.measurement: Log sequential tool call pairs; flag MCP→Read on same path as a fallback event
metrics.current_estimate: ~80% of MCP calls are followed by a redundant Read within 2 turns

metrics.target: >=95% of find_symbol queries return the correct definition as top result
metrics.measurement: Ground-truth eval: 200 randomly sampled symbol names → compare top-1 result to AST truth
metrics.current_estimate: ~70% for exact names; ~35% for partial/fuzzy names

metrics.measurement: Time each handler invocation; log p50/p95/p99 per tool

metrics.target: >=90% of agent queries answered without follow-up reads when using MCP tool
metrics.measurement: Agent eval: ask 50 standard codebase questions; score whether MCP response alone is sufficient
metrics.current_estimate: ~20% — most responses require at least one file read for verification

metrics.target: Index reflects file changes within 10 seconds (watcher mode) or reports staleness when older than 5 minutes
metrics.measurement: Compare max(file mtime) across all source files against index.lastBuilt
metrics.current_estimate: No staleness reporting; watcher_debounce=5s but no per-tool freshness signal

metrics.target: >=85% of file summaries rated 'useful' by agent (contain specific purpose, not just filename)
metrics.measurement: Automated scoring: flag summaries matching pattern '^\w+ module$' as low-quality
metrics.current_estimate: ~60% of summaries appear to be fallback 'X module' placeholders

agents.name: IndexOrchestrator
agents.role: Central controller for knowledge build and incremental update lifecycle. Coordinates parsing, summarization, call-graph construction, embedding generation, and quality gating. Triggered by file-system watcher events or explicit rebuild requests.
triggers: File watcher detects source file change (debounce 5s)
responsibilities: Determine which files changed since last build (mtime comparison)
Prioritize changed files for immediate re-indexing
Propagate changes through dependency graph (changed file A → re-index all files importing A)
Gate index publication behind Validator approval (quality score >= threshold)
Maintain buildGeneration counter for cache invalidation
Report build metrics: files parsed, symbols extracted, summaries generated, errors encountered

agents.name: FileParser
agents.role: Language-aware AST parser. Extracts symbols, call relationships, imports/exports, type signatures, decorators, and complexity metrics from source files. Runs as a sub-process invoked by IndexOrchestrator per file or batch.
extraction_targets: All function/method signatures with full parameter types and return types
All class definitions with inheritance chains
All call expressions with argument patterns
Line ranges (start line, end line) for all symbols — enabling code snippet extraction
edge_cases: Dynamic imports (import()): mark as 'dynamic' in dependency graph
Reflect-based calls: flag as 'dynamic-dispatch' in call graph
Barrel exports (index.ts re-exporting): trace through to original definition
Monorepo packages: treat each package.json scope as a separate module root
Circular imports: record and surface in index without infinite looping
Forward declarations (TypeScript declare): mark as 'ambient' type
Overloaded functions: index all overload signatures, link under same qualifiedName

output_schema.symbols: SymbolEntry[] (with startLine, endLine, codeSnippet fields added)

agents.name: LLMSummarizer
agents.role: Generates rich, context-aware summaries for files, modules, and symbols using an LLM. Replaces the static heuristic fallback ('X module') with substantive descriptions that answer: what this does, why it exists, how it fits the architecture, what callers need to know.

summarization_levels.minimal: One sentence: what the file does. Generated from imports/exports only, no LLM call.
summarization_levels.standard: 3-5 sentences covering purpose, key exports, notable dependencies, and patterns. LLM call with file content up to 4096 tokens.
summarization_levels.rich: Full structured summary including: purpose, architectural role, public API description, edge cases handled, performance characteristics, test coverage notes, usage examples from callers. LLM call with file content + caller context.

quality_gates: Summary must not match pattern '^\w+(-\w+)* module$' (filename-derived fallback)
Summary must reference at least one exported symbol by name
Summary must not contain placeholder text like 'TODO' or 'FIXME'
If summary fails quality gate, retry once with expanded prompt; if still failing, escalate to IndexOrchestrator
agents.prompt_strategy: Provide: (1) file content truncated to 3000 tokens, (2) list of callers from call graph, (3) list of symbols this file imports, (4) architecture.md context if available. Ask for: structured JSON with purpose, architecturalRole, publicAPI, internalPatterns, usageExamples.
agents.caching: Skip summarization if file contentHash unchanged and existing summary passes quality gate
agents.cost_control: Max 1000 tokens output per summary; batch up to 5 files per LLM call using structured multi-file prompt

agents.name: EmbeddingIndexer
agents.role: Generates vector embeddings for all file summaries, symbol docstrings, and module descriptions. Enables semantic search across the knowledge base — agents can query by concept rather than exact name.

index_format.format: HNSW flat file index (hnswlib-node or faiss)
index_format.metadata_store: Parallel JSON file mapping vector ID to {file, symbol, type, text}

agents.update_strategy: Incremental: only embed new or changed entries; use contentHash to detect changes
agents.semantic_search_integration: find_symbol tool gains optional 'semantic: true' parameter that queries embedding index instead of substring match

agents.name: CallGraphEnricher
agents.role: Enhances the call graph with call-site code snippets, argument patterns, and call frequency. Enables find_callers to return context-rich results that eliminate the need for follow-up file reads.
enrichment_fields: callSiteSnippet: 3 lines of source code around the call expression
argumentPattern: inferred types of arguments passed at this call site
callFrequency: how many times this symbol is called (total across all callers)
isTestCaller: boolean — caller is inside a test file
isInternalCaller: boolean — caller is within the same module
returnValueUsed: boolean — whether the return value is used at the call site
agents.output: enriched-call-graph.json: extends dependencies.json with per-call-site metadata

agents.name: IndexValidator
agents.role: Continuously monitors index quality by cross-checking indexed data against source files. Detects staleness, accuracy degradation, missing files, and parse failures. Reports to IndexOrchestrator and exposes quality metrics to health_check tool.

validation_checks.method: Compare index.lastBuilt against max(mtime) of all source files
validation_checks.threshold: Warn if index > 5 minutes older than newest source file
validation_checks.action: Emit staleness warning in tool responses; trigger incremental reindex

validation_checks.method: Randomly sample 10% of indexed symbols; re-parse source file and compare signature
validation_checks.threshold: Alert if >5% of sampled symbols have mismatched signatures
validation_checks.action: Escalate to IndexOrchestrator for full rebuild of affected files

validation_checks.method: Scan all summaries for quality gate failures (fallback pattern, too short)
validation_checks.threshold: Flag if >15% of summaries fail quality gate
validation_checks.action: Queue failed summaries for LLMSummarizer retry

validation_checks.method: Walk source file tree; verify every .ts/.js/.py/etc file has a summary entry
validation_checks.threshold: Alert if any source file lacks a summary
validation_checks.action: Add missing files to indexing queue

validation_checks.method: Verify calledBy[A] contains B iff calls[B] contains A (bidirectional invariant)
validation_checks.action: Rebuild call graph for affected symbols

validation_checks.method: Compare detected tech stack against package.json, tsconfig.json, *.config.* files
validation_checks.threshold: Alert if detected language differs from file extension distribution
validation_checks.action: Re-run tech stack detection with expanded heuristics

agents.name: QueryEnricher
agents.role: Post-processes raw index results before returning to calling agents. Adds inline code snippets, cross-references, freshness signals, and confidence scores to tool responses. Acts as a middleware layer between the index and the MCP tool output formatters.
enrichment_pipeline: 2. Attach code snippet (startLine to endLine + 2 lines of context)
3. Attach top-3 callers with call-site snippets
4. Attach top-3 callees with their one-line summaries
5. Compute and attach freshness signal (seconds since index was built vs file mtime)
6. Attach related files (imports, importedBy, siblings in same directory)
7. Attach usage examples from test files if available
8. Compute confidence score based on index age and validation status
9. Format response respecting dynamic budget (see response budget redesign)

agents.name: AdoptionAnalyzer
agents.role: Observes agent query patterns by parsing run logs. Identifies which queries cause MCP-to-file-read fallbacks, which symbols are most queried but poorly indexed, and which tools are underused. Produces weekly improvement recommendations for the IndexOrchestrator.
analysis_patterns: MCP call immediately followed by Read on same file path = fallback event
Grep tool used with a pattern matching a known symbol name = failed find_symbol
Glob tool used for source file discovery = failed get_project_overview
Sequential find_symbol + find_callers always paired = callers should be inlined
get_implementation_context + Read same file = response budget too small or missing snippet
agents.output: workspace/logs/adoption-report-{date}.json with: top-10 fallback triggers, recommended response budget adjustments, missing index fields, symbols needing richer summaries

tools.name: read_source_file
tools.description: Read a source file from the project root for parsing or summarization

tools.name: write_index_file
tools.description: Write or update a knowledge index file (symbols.json, dependencies.json, etc.)
tools.blast_radius: Knowledge base only — no source files modified

tools.name: run_ast_parser
tools.description: Invoke language-specific AST parser (TypeScript compiler API, tree-sitter, etc.) on a source file
tools.on_failure: Return partial parse result with error annotation; do not block indexing of other files

tools.name: call_embedding_api
tools.description: Generate vector embeddings for a list of text chunks
tools.on_failure: Skip embedding generation; log error; semantic search degrades to substring fallback

tools.name: emit_alert
tools.description: Write a quality alert to workspace/logs/alerts.jsonl

tools.name: read_run_logs
tools.description: Read JSONL run logs from workspace/logs/ for adoption analysis

current_issues: Tech stack returns 'Unknown' for TypeScript projects
No external dependency inventory (npm packages)
Entry points computed from calledBy count — misses actual entry points (main, bin, exports fields)
No architecture summary or module responsibility map
improvements: Fix tech stack detection: scan tsconfig.json, *.config.{ts,js,mjs}, file extension distribution across all indexed files
Add external_dependencies section: parse package.json dependencies + devDependencies, group by category (runtime/dev/peer)
Add entry_points from package.json main/bin/exports fields + any file named index.ts/main.ts
Add module_responsibilities: one-line role per module from module-level summary
Add dependency_graph_summary: list all module→module edges with 'A uses B for X' descriptions
Add circular_dependencies: explicit list of cycle members
Add index_freshness: seconds since last build, staleness warning if >300s
Increase response budget for this tool to 8000 bytes (it's the entry point — must be complete)

current_issues: Substring match only — misses typos, alternate casings, aliased exports
Returns up to 20 results but doesn't include callers/callees inline
No semantic search option
File path shown as absolute, not relative — hard to use in follow-up calls
improvements: Add fuzzy matching (Levenshtein distance <= 2) as fallback when substring match returns 0 results
Add semantic: boolean parameter — when true, query embedding index for conceptually similar symbols
Inline top-3 callers (name + file + line) in each result without requiring find_callers call
Inline top-3 callees in each result
Include code_snippet: 5-line snippet centered on the symbol definition
Show file as relative path from project root
Add is_exported, is_deprecated, test_coverage fields
Add overloads: list all overload signatures for overloaded functions
Add usage_examples: up to 2 call-site snippets from non-test callers
Increase result limit to 50 with pagination token for further results

current_issues: File purpose truncated to 60 characters — combined with hollow summaries yields '...' for most files
Exported symbols truncated to 20 entries with 80-char signature limit
No test file inventory
No external API surface for the module
No data flow summary
improvements: Remove 60-char truncation on file purposes; show full detailedPurpose (up to 200 chars)
Show all exported symbols without count cap; use pagination if >50
Add test_files section: list of test files that test this module
Add external_api section: all exported symbols that are imported by other modules (the public contract)
Add data_flow: describe how data enters and exits the module
Add change_impact: which other modules would be affected by changes to this module
Increase module response budget to 10000 bytes
Add per-file complexity scores in the files listing

current_issues: No code snippet — agents must Read the file after every call
Symbol signatures truncated to 80 chars
No backward-compatibility notes
No link to test files that test specific functions in this file
No usage examples showing how functions in this file are called
improvements: Add code_snippet field to each symbol: function body up to 40 lines (truncate with '...' if longer)
Remove 80-char signature truncation — return full signature
Add backward_compat_notes: detect if any exported symbols have @deprecated JSDoc tags
Add per-symbol test_coverage: names of test files and test cases covering each exported function
Add per-symbol usage_examples: up to 3 real call sites with 3-line snippets
Add file_code_overview: first 20 lines of the file as a quick orientation snippet
Increase response budget to 15000 bytes for this tool (it's the pre-edit context call)
Add related_tests: all test files that import this file

current_issues: Returns only qualifiedName + file + line — no call-site context
Cannot distinguish test callers from production callers
No argument pattern information
No call frequency (how commonly is this called?)
improvements: Add call_site_snippet: 3 lines surrounding the call expression
Add argument_pattern: inferred argument types at the call site
Add is_test_caller: boolean flag
Add is_internal_caller: boolean (same module vs cross-module)
Add return_value_used: boolean
Add call_frequency: total call count across all callers at all depths
Group results by: (1) direct callers, (2) transitive callers at depth 2+
Add caller_summary: one-line summary of the calling function's purpose

current_issues: Returns 'compact one-line summaries' which are often just the hollow fallback
Max 20 files per call — too low for module-level exploration
No indication of summary quality/confidence
improvements: Return quality_score per summary (high/medium/low based on quality gate results)
Increase max files to 50 per call
For low-quality summaries, include a note: 'Summary may be incomplete — call get_implementation_context for richer detail'
Add symbol_count per file in the response
Add last_modified timestamp per file

current_issues: Returns basic stats but no quality metrics
No per-tool latency data
No adoption rate metrics
Verbose mode builds file tree on-the-fly (slow)
improvements: Add quality_metrics section from quality-metrics.json
Add index_freshness: age in seconds, staleness flag if >300s
Add adoption_rate from AdoptionAnalyzer reports
Add per-tool: call_count, avg_latency_ms, fallback_rate from last 24h
Add parse_errors: count and list of files with parse failures
Add embedding_index_status: present/missing/stale

current_issues: Searches only architecture.md — a single file
Keyword grep only — no semantic understanding
Returns ±3 lines context — often insufficient
improvements: Expand search corpus: include all file summaries + module descriptions + artifact JSONs
Add semantic: true parameter to search via embedding index
Increase context window to ±10 lines
Add source_type field to each result (architecture_doc / file_summary / module_description)
Support multi-term queries with AND/OR logic

index_storage.format: JSON files on disk in .knowledge/ directory

files.index.json: KnowledgeIndex — metadata, module list, build info, quality metrics
files.symbols.json: SymbolEntry[] — all symbols with enhanced fields
files.dependencies.json: DependencyGraph — module and file-level dependency edges
files.embeddings.json: Record<id, {vector: number[], metadata: EmbeddingMetadata}> — semantic search index
files.code-snippets.json: Record<qualifiedName, {snippet: string, startLine: number, endLine: number}> — indexed code snippets
files.enriched-call-graph.json: EnrichedCallGraph — call edges with site snippets and argument patterns
files.external-deps.json: ExternalDependencyMap — npm/pip/cargo packages with versions and usage locations
files.quality-metrics.json: QualityMetrics — accuracy scores, coverage stats, adoption rates

index_storage.locking: File-level write lock using .knowledge/.build.lock; readers proceed during build using previous generation
index_storage.versioning: buildGeneration counter in index.json; readers check generation matches before trusting cached results

symbol_coverage: All functions (named, arrow, anonymous assigned to const/let)
All module-level constants that are exported

relationship_coverage.type_hierarchy: Class extends/implements chains; interface extends chains
relationship_coverage.data_flow: Heuristic: track where return values from key functions are assigned
relationship_coverage.module_boundaries: Explicit boundary at module root (top-level directory)

metadata_per_file: complexityScore (sum of cyclomatic complexity of all functions)

incremental_update_strategy.change_detection: Compare current mtime + contentHash against index entries
incremental_update_strategy.propagation: When file A changes: re-index A + all files that import A (one-hop reverse dependency expansion)
incremental_update_strategy.batch_size: Process up to 20 files per incremental cycle to avoid blocking queries
incremental_update_strategy.priority_queue: Changed files first, then their importers, then full rebuild of call graph
incremental_update_strategy.atomic_publish: Write to .knowledge/.next/ then atomically rename to .knowledge/ after build completes
incremental_update_strategy.fallback: If incremental fails, trigger full rebuild

memory_management.in_memory_cache: LRU cache of last 100 queried symbols and 50 file summaries (per process)
memory_management.cache_invalidation: On each new buildGeneration, flush in-memory cache
memory_management.large_codebase_handling: Paginate symbol results; stream large files through AST parser without loading full content into memory

memory.short_term: Per-build context: list of changed files, parse errors, build progress. Held in IndexOrchestrator memory for duration of one build cycle.
memory.long_term: Persistent index files in .knowledge/ directory. Summaries include contentHash for change detection. Quality metrics accumulate across builds.
memory.episodic: workspace/logs/adoption-report-{date}.json — tracks which queries caused fallbacks, which symbols are most queried, which summaries are consistently rated low quality by AdoptionAnalyzer.
memory.context_management: IndexOrchestrator maintains a build manifest in memory listing all in-progress file tasks. Completed tasks are written to disk immediately. No full codebase held in memory simultaneously.
memory.agent_query_memory: Query patterns logged in workspace/logs/run-{id}.jsonl with tool name, params, latency, and whether a file read followed within 2 turns (fallback signal).

escalation_triggers: 3 consecutive file parse failures in a row — pause parsing, alert IndexOrchestrator
Summary quality score drops below 70% — trigger full re-summarization
Index staleness exceeds 10 minutes despite watcher being active — alert and rebuild
Embedding API returns error for >5 consecutive calls — disable semantic search, fall back to substring
Build takes > 10 minutes — emit warning, continue but log slow build
Index file write fails (disk full, permissions) — abort build, preserve previous index
Query latency p99 exceeds 2000ms — alert, investigate slow index loading
human_approval_required_for: Disabling a tool due to accuracy issues (requires approval before returning isError on valid queries)
Major index schema changes that would invalidate all cached summaries
Adding support for a new programming language (AST parser integration)

safety_boundaries.source_files: Read-only — indexing pipeline never modifies source files
safety_boundaries.knowledge_directory: Read-write for index files only — never writes outside .knowledge/
safety_boundaries.external_network: Embedding API calls only — no other outbound network access during indexing

error_handling.missing_index: Return degraded response with message indicating staleness; attempt live scan of source file as fallback for single-file queries
error_handling.parse_failure: Log error, skip symbol extraction for that file, return partial results with parse_error flag
error_handling.corrupt_index: Detect via JSON parse error on load; trigger rebuild; return 'index rebuilding' message to agents
error_handling.stale_index: Include index_age_seconds in all tool responses; flag as STALE if > 300s; agents can decide whether to trust
error_handling.concurrent_access: Build process uses .build.lock; query tools read previous generation while new generation builds
error_handling.timeout: Per-file 10s parse timeout; if exceeded, log and skip; do not block other files
error_handling.embedding_api_unavailable: Gracefully degrade to substring search; log availability miss; retry after 60s backoff

response_budget_redesign.principle: Dynamic budget based on query type and agent context. Rich queries get large budgets; lightweight listing queries stay compact.
response_budget_redesign.implementation: Replace MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES = 3800 constant with a per-tool configurable limit (see guardrails.max_response_bytes_by_tool)
prioritization_rules: Priority 0 (always included): file/symbol identity, location, type
Priority 1 (included unless budget exhausted): purpose/summary, signature
Priority 2 (include if budget allows): code_snippet, callers/callees
Priority 3 (include if budget allows): usage examples, related files
Priority 4 (include if budget allows): patterns, metrics, metadata
response_budget_redesign.pagination: Add next_page_token to responses that exceed budget; agent can request next page without re-querying index

success_criteria: MCP tool adoption rate >= 90% (agents use MCP as first call for codebase queries)
Follow-up file read rate after MCP call <= 10% (down from ~80%)
Symbol resolution accuracy >= 95% for exact name queries
Symbol resolution accuracy >= 80% for fuzzy/semantic queries
Query response latency < 500ms p99 for find_symbol, get_file_summary
Query response latency < 1000ms p99 for get_implementation_context, find_callers
Summary quality score >= 85% (no hollow fallback summaries)
Index coverage >= 99% of source files within 30 seconds of change detection
Zero cases of get_project_overview returning 'Unknown' for a TypeScript project

test_scenario_categories.description: Query known symbols by exact name, partial name, typo variant, and semantic description. Verify top-1 result is correct.
test_cases: find_symbol('handler') → should return all handler functions with correct files
find_symbol('loadSymbol') → should fuzzy-match to 'loadSymbols'
find_symbol('file reader', semantic=true) → should return read_source_file or similar
find_symbol('HandlerFunc', type='function') → cross-language test (Go)
find_symbol('') → should return graceful error, not crash

test_scenario_categories.description: Call get_implementation_context on various files. Verify agent can answer standard questions without a follow-up Read.
test_cases: get_implementation_context('tools/find-symbol.ts') → agent should answer 'what parameters does find_symbol accept?' without Read
get_implementation_context('server.ts') → agent should list all registered tools without Read
get_implementation_context('types.ts') → agent should describe SymbolEntry shape without Read
get_implementation_context('tools/lib/response-budget.ts') → agent should know the budget constant without Read
get_implementation_context on a file with 500+ lines → verify pagination works and response is still useful

test_scenario_categories.description: Query module context and verify agent understands module responsibilities and boundaries.
test_cases: get_module_context('tools') → should describe all 19 tool files with meaningful purposes
get_module_context('tools/lib') → should describe 6 library files with their roles
get_module_context('mcp-server') → should describe the top-level server module
get_module_context with non-existent module → should return helpful list of available modules

test_scenario_categories.description: Test find_callers returns enough context for agents to understand call patterns without file reads.
test_cases: find_callers('handler', maxDepth=2) → should show call-site snippets for all call locations
find_callers('loadIndex') → should distinguish test callers from production callers
find_callers('buildResponse') → should show which tools call buildResponse and with what arguments
find_callers('nonExistentSymbol') → should return helpful error with find_symbol suggestion

test_scenario_categories.description: Verify get_project_overview returns accurate tech stack and complete module map.
test_cases: get_project_overview() on TypeScript project → Languages must include 'TypeScript', not 'Unknown'
get_project_overview() → must list all npm dependencies with versions
get_project_overview(depth=3) → must include all 26 source files in tree

test_scenario_categories.description: Test system behavior under degraded conditions.
test_cases: Stale index (> 5 minutes old) → tools should include STALE warning in response
Corrupt symbols.json → find_symbol should return error with rebuild instruction, not crash
File deleted after indexing → get_implementation_context should note file_not_found, suggest rebuild
Circular dependency in code → get_dependencies should surface cycle information without infinite loop

failure_modes_tested: Index out of sync (file modified, index not rebuilt) — verify staleness signal propagates
Missing symbols for newly added file — verify watcher triggers incremental update
Circular dependencies causing infinite BFS — verify loop detection
Polyglot repo (TS + Python) — verify both languages indexed correctly
Large monorepo (5000+ files) — verify performance and pagination
Stale cache used despite fresh files — verify contentHash invalidation
Concurrent index write + read — verify read gets consistent view
LLM summarizer API failure — verify graceful fallback to static summary
Embedding API rate limit — verify semantic search falls back to substring
Symbol overloads — verify all signatures indexed, correct one returned
Parse error in one file — verify other files indexed successfully
Adversarial input to find_symbol (SQL injection patterns, path traversal) — verify sanitization

benchmark_approach.baseline: Record MCP vs Read/Grep/Glob call ratios across 10 agent sessions before improvements
benchmark_approach.post_improvement: Record same ratios after each major improvement phase
benchmark_approach.ground_truth_eval: For symbol accuracy: manually curate 200 {query, expected_symbol} pairs; automate weekly evaluation
benchmark_approach.agent_blind_test: Run 20 standard codebase tasks with agent using only MCP tools (no Read/Grep); measure task completion rate and quality

phase_1.name: Critical Path Fixes (Week 1-2)
items: Fix tech stack detection for TypeScript/JavaScript projects (tsconfig.json detection)
Replace hollow summaries: improve LLMSummarizer prompt to require specific content, add quality gate
Increase response budgets per tool (from universal 3800 to tool-specific limits)
Add code_snippet field to find_symbol and get_implementation_context responses
Inline top-3 callers in find_symbol results (eliminate mandatory find_callers follow-up)
Fix file path normalization: return relative paths consistently
Add index_age_seconds and is_stale fields to all tool responses

phase_2.name: Richness Expansion (Week 3-4)
items: Implement CallGraphEnricher: add call-site snippets and argument patterns to find_callers
Add external-deps.json: npm/pip package inventory with versions
Implement IndexValidator: quality metrics, staleness detection, accuracy sampling
Add per-symbol usage_examples from test files
Implement response pagination with next_page_token
Expand get_module_context: test_files, external_api, change_impact sections
Add file line ranges to FileParser output to enable precise code snippet extraction

phase_3.name: Semantic Intelligence (Week 5-6)
items: Implement EmbeddingIndexer: vector index for all summaries and docstrings
Add semantic: true parameter to find_symbol
Add fuzzy match fallback (Levenshtein) to find_symbol
Implement AdoptionAnalyzer: parse run logs, identify fallback patterns
Expand search_architecture to cover all summaries, not just architecture.md
Add multi-language support (Python, Go) to FileParser
Add health_check quality_metrics and adoption_rate sections

phase_4.name: Observability & Continuous Improvement (Week 7-8)
items: Implement per-tool latency logging in server.ts
Set up automated weekly symbol accuracy evaluation
Implement AdoptionAnalyzer weekly report generation
Add monitoring dashboard for index quality metrics
Implement automatic re-summarization of low-quality summaries on each rebuild
Add type hierarchy indexing (class extends/implements chains)

metrics_to_track: fallback_events_count (MCP call followed by Read on same file)
index_freshness_seconds (age of index at query time)
monitoring_and_observability.log_format: JSONL, one event per line, written to workspace/logs/mcp-metrics-{date}.jsonl
alerting: adoption_rate_pct < 70% → alert: MCP tools may be regressing
summary_quality_score_pct < 75% → alert: LLM summarizer may be failing
index_freshness_seconds > 600 → alert: watcher may be stuck
parse_error_count > 10 in one build → alert: potential AST parser issue
tool_latency_p99_ms > 2000 → alert: index loading is too slow
dashboards: MCP adoption rate over time (agents preferring MCP vs file reads)
Per-tool call frequency and latency distribution
Top 10 fallback triggers (which queries cause file reads)

architecture_diagram.description: Orchestrator pattern with 4 specialized sub-agents coordinated by IndexOrchestrator
data_flow: MCP Tool (e.g. find_symbol) → QueryEnricher → .knowledge/ → enriched response → Agent
Agent run logs → AdoptionAnalyzer → improvement recommendations → IndexOrchestrator
